inline_comments.message:
General comment - use camel case as per Google java style guide see (https://google.github.io/styleguide/javaguide.html#s5.3-camel-case)
i.e. AsdValidator.

probably also need to override the validateManifestFile method - I would expect SOL004ManifestOnboarding:: processBody to fail on the new manifest entries

Adding a package to the integration tests in ASD format will verify if any other impacts missing
also add the other new manifest metadata entries

Latest input is that a decision has been taken to drop the "oran" prefix from all new manifest entries (i.e. entry_definition_type instead of oran_entry_definition_type), same for oran_application_name which now becomes application_name etc. See: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Application+Service+Descriptor+%28ASD%29+Onboarding+Packaging+Format
Rename fields, method names etc in other classes as well to remove "oran"

Maybe I'm missing something, but I couldn't find the specific validation of the other entries application_name, application_provider and release_date_time, as specified here https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Application+Service+Descriptor+%28ASD%29+Onboarding+Packaging+Format
For better clarity, I would recommend to go with the common if instead of nested ternary operators
same comment here for the lower camel case hasAsdMetadata
The ASD types are being added to the SDC model (for now at least) so this can be removed
this should be ETSI_ENTRY_CERTIFICATE, i.e. same as in the base class
these wont be in the TOSCA.meta
create a method getOnboardingManifest() which can be overridden in subclasses
why not use the existing getManifest method?
This seems to be a lot of duplication of code between this class and SOL004ManifestOnboarding
dont seem to validate for asd, e.g no pnf entries
ASD metadata entries should only be valid for ASDs
These wont be in the TOSCA.meta onboarded, they will be in the manifest so dont belong here

because this one is used by isAsdPackage, and isAsdPackage is used in ValidatorFactory::getValidator, and if i used the existing getManifest i got an exception and wasnt returning the proper validator... we could have a deeper think about it, i also dont like it as its like repeating too much code for the same...
This change still there in the latest code.
I suggest to rename to onboardingManifest, this is not the type itself, but the instance. Also to remove the redundant variable in the line 325.
I suggest to remove the redundant else to reduce the code complexity
you are repeating this call in every condition. You should move this to the top, before the initial if, on line 51, and remove all other calls
this is not related to ETSI. It would be better to move to another place with more correlation if possible.

the token attribute is there to represent the entry, avoiding the use of the enum name. It is better for maintenance when you don't rely on the enum name, so a change in the name will not break the business logic.
same here for ASD related methods, we should move to another class as this is not related to ETSI
Do we need those entries? I could not find anything in this change related to those prefixes.
why not call the getOnboardingManifest() here and implement it in the child classes?

could be simplified to:
null != manifest && manifest.getMetadata().entrySet().stream()
            .anyMatch(manifestEntry -> ENTRY_DEFINITION_TYPE.getToken().equalsIgnoreCase(manifestEntry.getKey()))
I would say that this method has no effective difference from getManifestInputStream, is there a particular reason to not use getManifestInputStream instead?
i tried to use the original at the begining but it was throwing exception 'manifest not found' when used by ValidatorFactory::getValidator, preventing to get the right validator...

talking to Javier, the "org.onap.policy.clamp.controlloop." entry was from a previous rebase, then the configuration changed after that. He just removed.
The other entry he said it is necessary for the ASD package.

please, adjust formatting, it is breaking the line limit. I would suggest:

getManifestMetadata(metadata).stream()
            .filter(requiredEntry -> !metadata.containsKey(requiredEntry) || (ENTRY_DEFINITION_TYPE.getToken().equalsIgnoreCase(requiredEntry)
                && !isValidEntryDefinitionType(requiredEntry, metadata.get(requiredEntry))))
            .forEach(requiredEntry ->
                reportError(ErrorLevel.ERROR, String.format(Messages.MANIFEST_METADATA_MISSING_ENTRY.getErrorMessage(), requiredEntry))
            );

this method and getAsdMetadata are not manifest related. I would rather put in a separate class, maybe "AsdPackageHelper".
It is just a suggestion, what do you think?
would be great to add unit tests for this class
No need to override methods when the impl is the same as the base class
note sure we need all the checks on isAsdPackage - just call before the if on line 53 and remove from here?
Should also check if the manifest entry is valid for ASD, i.e isMetadataAsdEntry()
does thie method need to be overwritten?
Looks like there is a lot of duplication of code between this class and ETSIServiceImpl. Please re-work to have common code in one class which is reused in both locations

please, log also the exception to not loose it:
LOGGER.warn("Manifest file not found", ioe);

I think you took my comment up wrong, this class should not use AsdPackageHelper class, the code that was repeated in AsdPackageHelper and ETSIServiceImpl should be extracted to a separate class that is used from both AsdPackageHelper and ETSIServiceImpl
Add javadoc to the class and public methods
'handler' is too vague (handler of what) rename to fileContentHandler
rename abstratManifest to manifestHandler, same comment in other methods
doesnt appear to serve any prupose
